title: The Bayon of Angkor Thom: An Architectural Model of Buddhist Cosmology
highlights: Bayon of Angkor Thom: An Architectural Model of Buddhist Cosmology  ...    The paper explores the architectural significance of the Bayon at Angkor Thom, emphasizing its embodiment of Buddhist cosmology. By examining its historical context, developmental phases of the Khmer Empire, and unique structural features, the discussion illustrates how the Bayon serves not only as a religious monument but also as a representation  ...    * The Bayon is the primary Buddhist structure at Angkor, and was the architectural embodiment of the Buddhist concept  ...    * The galleries that enclose the central sanctuary represent a mandala, or the levels of the universe, and since the Bayon was built inside Angkor Thom, the outer galleries were eliminated. * The mysterious colossal faces of the Bayon, with the trademark "Angkor smile" provokes debate as to their  ...    universal worlds.
